2604.15153.title: Compressing Sequences in the Latent Embedding Space: $K$-Token Merging for Large Language Models
2604.15153.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) incur significant computational and memory costs when processing long prompts, as full self-attention scales quadratically with input length. Token compression aims to address this challenge by reducing the number of tokens representing inputs. However, existing prompt-compression approaches primarily operate in token space and overlook inefficiencies in the latent embedding space. In this paper, we propose K-Token Merging, a latent-space compression framework that merges each contiguous block of K token embeddings into a single embedding via a lightweight encoder. The compressed sequence is processed by a LoRA-adapted LLM, while generation remains in the original vocabulary. Experiments on structural reasoning (Textualized Tree), sentiment classification (Amazon Reviews), and code editing (CommitPackFT) show that K-Token Merging lies on the Pareto frontier of performance vs. compression, achieving up to 75% input length reduction with minimal performance degradation.
2604.15153.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在處理長提示時會產生顯著的計算和記憶體成本，因為完整的自注意力隨著輸入長度的增加而呈平方級增長。<lb/>令牌壓縮旨在通過減少表示輸入的令牌數量來解決這一挑戰。<lb/>然而，現有的提示壓縮方法主要在令牌空間中運作，忽視了潛在嵌入空間中的低效。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了K-Token Merging，一種潛在空間壓縮框架，通過輕量級編碼器將每個連續的K個令牌嵌入合併為單個嵌入。<lb/>壓縮後的序列由經過LoRA調整的LLM處理，而生成仍然使用原始詞彙。<lb/>在結構推理（Textualized Tree）、情感分類（Amazon Reviews）和代碼編輯（CommitPackFT）上的實驗顯示，K-Token Merging位於性能與壓縮的Pareto邊界上，實現了高達75%的輸入長度減少，且性能降級最小。
2604.15153.author: Zihao Xu et.al.
2604.15153.authors: Zihao Xu, John Harvill, Ziwei Fan, Yizhou Sun, Hao Ding, Hao Wang
